briefing.headline: Rig up a Chatterbait — steady pre-spawn approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Chatterbait is a strong choice for pre-spawn with muddy water and stable conditions.

KVD: Thunder Cricket in shallow grass even in upper 30s water. Use 3/8 oz (lighter than normal) with straight-tail blade minnow trailer for subtle action. Pull and stop, not steady retrieve. Get it down in old dead vegetation — it holds heat and bass are drawn to it. Tungsten model has smaller blade

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: Thunder Cricket in shallow grass even in upper 30s water. Use 3/8 oz (lighter than normal) with straight-tail blade minnow trailer for subtle action. Pull and stop, not steady retrieve. Get it do
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, rock, ledge. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: Post-frontal cold pre-spawn — work the Thunder Cricket like a jig. Get it down in the grass, hop it, twitch it. Let it hit bottom or grass, then twitch. 'Sometimes you got to make them react.' Bi
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, rock, ledge. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: In cold/post-frontal pre-spawn, don't just wind the Thunder Cricket. Work it like a jig — hop, twitch, pull-and-stop. Get it down in the grass and on the bottom. Bites are extremely subtle: the bait just gets heavy or the vibration stops. 'I ...
Wheeler: For a spring pre-spawn event limited to three rods, picks a jerkbait/crankbait for deeper staging fish, a 1/2-oz jig for versatility, and a ChatterBait for stained shallow water.
Yamamoto pro Hite: Standard Zako for ChatterBaits (subtle natural action), Kickin' Zako (paddle tail) for deep heavy ChatterBaits on ledges with poor light penetration and for swim jigs exclusively

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable pre-spawn conditions mean fish are staging predictably at 5-12ft on points, channel swings, and riprap banks. They're feeding aggressively as water warms. Work the transition zone between deep winter haunts and shallow spawning areas.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
